title: Find every genuine bug in the inventory service
judgeNotes: Answer key — 8 planted bugs: (1) __init__ mutable default `items=[]` shared across instances; (2) add() mutates self.audit outside the lock (race on list append/iteration); (3) total() iterates self.items without the lock while writers mutate it (RuntimeError/torn reads); (4) reserve() is check-then-act: total() outside the same critical section as the append, two threads can both reserve the last stock (TOCTOU); (5) remove_zero_rows() deletes from the list while iterating with enumerate, skipping the element after every deletion; (6) load() swallows every exception including corrupt JSON and wrong types, silently keeping stale/partial state; (7) merge_inventories aliases and mutates a.items instead of copying; (8) restock_all appends to svc.items (via add) while iterating it — infinite loop. Bonus minor (+2, not required): `qty < 0` guard contradicts the "must be positive" message, allowing qty=0. Scoring /100: recall 8×9=72; fix quality 16 (fixes minimal and correct, e.g. reserve fixed by holding one lock across check+append); precision 12, minus 4 per clear false positive (nonexistent issue stated as a bug), floor 0. Restating one bug twice is not a false positive.
